title: Quiz: Algorithmic Trading Fundamentals
description: Test your understanding of algorithmic trading basics

questions.question: What is algorithmic trading?

options.text: Using computer programs to automatically execute trades based on predefined rules

questions.explanation: Algorithmic trading uses computer programs to automatically execute trades when specific conditions are met, removing emotional decision-making and ensuring consistent strategy execution.

questions.question: What advantage do high-frequency traders primarily rely on?
questions.explanation: High-frequency traders rely on speed and cutting-edge infrastructure to execute trades in microseconds to seconds, often profiting from small price discrepancies before others can react.

questions.question: Which strategy category typically has the highest win rate?
questions.explanation: Arbitrage strategies typically have the highest win rates (80-95%) because they exploit price discrepancies that are mathematically certain to converge. However, they require high capital and sophisticated infrastructure.

questions.question: What does the Sharpe ratio measure?
questions.explanation: The Sharpe ratio measures risk-adjusted returns by comparing excess return (above risk-free rate) to volatility. A higher Sharpe ratio indicates better return per unit of risk.

questions.question: Trend following strategies typically have low win rates but high average winners.
questions.explanation: True. Trend following strategies often have win rates of only 30-45%, but their winning trades are typically much larger than losing trades, resulting in positive expected value overall.

questions.question: Which quantum computing advantage is most applicable to portfolio optimization?
questions.explanation: Quantum optimization algorithms like QAOA (Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm) can potentially provide speedups for portfolio optimization problems by exploring solution spaces more efficiently.

questions.question: What does 'profit factor' measure?

options.text: Total profits divided by number of trades
Gross profits divided by gross losses

questions.explanation: Profit factor is the ratio of gross profits to gross losses. A profit factor above 1.0 indicates the strategy makes more than it loses, with values above 1.5 generally considered good.

questions.question: Which are examples of mean reversion signals? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Mean reversion signals identify when prices have deviated from their average: RSI extremes, Bollinger band touches, and z-score extremes all indicate potential reversion. Moving average crossovers are trend-following signals.
